public_reputation: Quiet dock necromancer paid to name wreck-dead before claims are filed.
manner: Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In Cosmouth, that reads as someone maritime, urbane, and commercially fluid, balancing archive-minded memory with port pragmatism, and the habits of a necromancers still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of necromancers in and around Coralhaven Port, Cosmouth, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Coralhaven Port, Cosmouth workable through the obligations of necromancers while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Cosmouth.
hidden_pressure: Must stay useful at the seam where policy becomes ordinary consequence. Around Coralhaven Port, Cosmouth, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of necromancers visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Brine-Caller Edda Silt can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Brine-Caller Edda Silt can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained.
public_signal: Brine-Caller Edda Silt carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Coralhaven Port, Cosmouth: the part of Coralhaven Port, Cosmouth where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break
